sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Abdominal pain
History of Present Illness
Karen reports abdominal pain that started approximately ten days ago with mild discomfort on her right side, which has been progressively worsening over the past couple of days. She describes the pain as generally around a four or five on a scale, but occasionally reaching a seven or eight. The pain is exacerbated by meals, particularly after eating, and seems to worsen when she's in certain positions. She reports some nausea and mild heartburn, but no vomiting. She notes that her appetite has decreased slightly and she feels more fatigued. She has been using excedrin migraine when needed for migraines and sumatriptan occasionally. She has not been checking her blood pressures daily. She reports chills but no fever. She mentions a trip to Europe last summer and no recent travel.
Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness in the right upper quadrant, with a Murphys sign being equivocal. A grade 3 systolic ejection murmur is heard. Blood pressure is elevated at 128/78. The remainder of the physical exam is unremarkable.
Assessment
The patient's presentation is concerning for possible gallbladder pathology, including cholecystitis or gallstones. Further investigation is warranted.
Plan
medical_concepts: tenderness in the right upper quadrant

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I have ordered a right upper quadrant ultrasound, a c-reactive protein test, and a complete blood count. I will also prescribe a refill of lisinopril 20mg daily. I will also prescribe sumatriptan as needed. I will follow up with you in four weeks, or sooner if needed based on the results of the tests.

medical_concepts: tenderness in the right upper quadrant
